arts.ttl: Torino and Atalanta Target Empoli’s Tino Anjorin
arts.sum: Torino and Atalanta are both showing interest in Tino Anjorin, the talented midfielder currently playing for Empoli. According to Gianluca di Marzio, he 22-year-old, who joined Empoli from Chelsea last summer, has caught the attention of these Serie A clubs as they look to strengthen their midfields. The post Torino and Atalanta Target Empoli’s Tino Anjorin appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Udinese v Atalanta: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Atalanta's attacking lineup receives a boost as their leading goalscorer Mateo Retegui resumes training following an injury layoff. While he may not start, Retegui could potentially feature in today's match against Udinese. Veteran wing-back Juan Cuadrado has also recovered from a thigh issue, adding depth to the squad. arts.ttl: Atalanta to visit Udine for the lead
arts.sum: Atalanta have not lost any of the last 14 matches against Udinese in Serie A (W9 D5): in the history of the competition, only against Sassuolo has the Nerazzurri played 15 matches in a row without defeat, between 2014 and 2021. Since 2016/17 (beginning of the Gasperini era in Bergamo), Atalanta have lost only one of eight away matches against Udinese in Serie A (W3 D4) – on the eight previous occasions, the Nerazzurri had lost five matches on the road.

arts.ttl: Serie A Matchday 20 Betting Preview: Udinese vs Atalanta
arts.sum: Saturday's Serie A action gets underway at the Bluenergy Stadium as mid-table Udinese entertain Scudetto hopefuls Atalanta. Though 16 points separate the teams ahead of the kick-off, the Friulians can be a thorn in Atalanta's side. arts.ttl: Alexis Sanchez made it perfectly clear where his loyalties lie between Arsenal and Man Utd
arts.sum: Manchester United make the trip to Arsenal on Sunday as two English giants revive their FA Cup rivalry - and Alexis Sanchez has already made it clear who he'll be supporting

arts.ttl: Udinese Pursue Mads Roerslev from Brentford
arts.sum: Udinese are pursuing a new addition to their squad, with Danish right-back Mads Roerslev of Brentford emerging as a key target. Following a successful start to the season, where the team currently sits in ninth place in the Serie A standings, Udinese aims to strengthen their defensive line as they look to build on their momentum. The post Udinese Pursue Mads Roerslev from Brentford appeared first on FootItalia.com.
